headline: Brain Drain: 1.86 Million Malaysians Abroad with 78% Non-Bumiputera
context: World Bank data shows 1.86 million Malaysians live abroad, 78% non-Bumiputera, with 62% citing 'limited opportunities' and 'unequal treatment' as primary emigration reasons.

cards.big: 1.86 million Malaysians live abroad. 78% are non-Bumiputera.
cards.sub: 62% cite unequal opportunity as the reason. This is not migration — it is a verdict on the system.

cards.big: Malaysia loses an estimated RM48B annually in foregone economic output.
cards.sub: The diaspora's aggregate income exceeds RM120B. They earn it elsewhere.

cards.big: TalentCorp's Returning Expert Programme has attracted 6,400 returnees since 2011.
cards.sub: In the same period, 340,000 new emigrants left. The inflow is 1.9% of the outflow.
Singapore's gain is directly correlated with Malaysia's equality deficit.

cards.big: The country's most educated citizens are voting with their feet. The exit poll says more than any survey.
Brain drain is the market's response to structural inequality. Addressing it requires addressing the inequality — not just offering tax incentives to return.
